checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2012, prior-year comparative cash flow statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?
Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=11487000 (2013 Q1); NetIncomeLoss=8457000 (2013 Q2); NetIncomeLoss=23726000 (2013 Q3); NetIncomeLoss=67489000 (2013 Q4)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?
Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: UPLOAD filing_date=2013-09-17; CORRESP filing_date=2013-09-24; UPLOAD filing_date=2013-10-23
evidence.location: filing_chronology (SEC comment-letter correspondence cluster around FY2013 10-K review)

evidence.quote: UPLOAD filing_date=2015-07-02; CORRESP filing_date=2015-07-13; UPLOAD filing_date=2015-08-04
evidence.location: filing_chronology (SEC comment-letter correspondence cluster shortly before cutoff)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Series span Assets, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, SalesRevenueNet, NetIncomeLoss, NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities, AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent from 2007/2008 through 2015-06-30 with quarterly and annual granularity

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Bad-debt/doubtful-accounts allowance was reduced to its lowest level in the observed history (concept=AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=338000 (2013-12-31)) even as gross receivables grew ~22% (concept=AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=559763000 (2013-12-31) vs 459081000 (2012-12-31)). This under-reserving pattern would understate bad-debt expense and overstate net receivables/net income in FY2013, which is consistent with the unusually large Q4 2013 net income spike (concept=NetIncomeLoss=67489000 (2013 Q4) vs ~8-24M in prior 2013 quarters). The subsequent ~8.7x increase in the allowance (concept=AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2943000 (2014-09-30)) suggests a catch-up/true-up of reserves in the following year.

Receivables grew ~21.9% year-over-year in FY2013 (concept=AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=559763000 (2013-12-31) vs 459081000 (2012-12-31)) while full-year revenue grew only ~1.55% (concept=SalesRevenueNet=2506467000 (2013) vs 2468150000 (2012)), with the imbalance concentrated in a large Q4 2013 revenue and receivables jump (concept=SalesRevenueNet=756843000 (Q4 2013) vs ~599658000 (Q3 2013); concept=AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=559763000 (2013-12-31) vs 423564000 (2013-09-30)). This pattern is consistent with either purchase-accounting effects of a late-2013 acquisition (goodwill rose from concept=Goodwill=37912000 (2013-09-30) to 44691000 (2013-12-31)) or with aggressive period-end revenue recognition/extended credit terms; the data alone cannot distinguish between the two, but the magnitude and timing warrant scrutiny of revenue cutoff practices.

In FY2010, net income substantially exceeded operating cash flow (concept=NetIncomeLoss=79758000 (2010) vs concept=NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=5166000 (2010)), a gap of roughly $74.6 million driven by working-capital build (inventory rose from concept=InventoryNet=315743000 (2009-12-31) to 361570000 (2010-12-31); receivables rose from concept=AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=417268000 (2009-12-31) to 455930000 (2010-12-31)). While partly explainable by post-recession demand recovery, the scale of the NI/OCF gap raises the possibility that earnings were not fully supported by cash generation in that period, warranting closer review of revenue/receivable and inventory recognition timing.

top_signals: AR growth (21.9% YoY at FY2013) vastly outpaced revenue growth (1.55% YoY), concentrated in an unusually large Q4 2013 spike

Allowance for doubtful accounts fell to a multi-year low (338,000 at FY2013-end) even as receivables grew sharply, then jumped ~8.7x by Q3 2014 (2,943,000), suggesting possible under-reserving followed by a catch-up adjustment

Q4 2013 net income (67,489,000) was 3-8x higher than other 2013 quarters, coinciding with the AR/revenue spike and a goodwill increase (37,912,000 to 44,691,000) suggesting a late-year acquisition that complicates but does not fully explain the mismatch
FY2010 showed a large gap between net income (79,758,000) and operating cash flow (5,166,000), driven by working-capital build
Recurring SEC comment-letter correspondence (UPLOAD/CORRESP filings) clustered around FY2013 and again just before the 2015 cutoff, indicating elevated regulatory scrutiny
